section: Research, Development, and Science & Medicine Analysis
key_findings: Canada ranks 1st in G7 for AI talent concentration growth and AI papers per capita every year since 2019
Canada holds 10% of the world's top 0.5% AI researchers — ranked 3rd globally
Canada produced only 1 notable AI model in 2024 vs USA (40), China (15), France (3)
Canada's patent output (0.17 per 100K) is 30x lower than USA (5.20) and 100x lower than South Korea (17.27)
Country AI index: Infrastructure 77, Government Strategy 100, but Research 31, Development 26, Commercial 15
Industry now produces 90.2% of notable AI models globally — Canada lacks sufficient industry-scale R&D
China's strategic choice: open-source models (DeepSeek, Qwen); US: proprietary API models (GPT, Claude, Gemini)
Canada excels at knowledge generation but fails to move projects through TRL 4-8 (validation, prototyping, pilot deployment)
Science & Medicine score: Canada ranks 6th in comparison group, clustered with UK, Singapore, Germany, France
core_gap: Canada has strong research supply (talent, publications, citations) but fails to translate into patents, notable models, or commercial products. The challenge is moving AI projects through the mid-to-late innovation stages rather than generating more upstream research.
